title: T-056 — Reference.Departments RLS hotfix follow-up + test contract
prompt:
# T-056 — Reference.Departments RLS hotfix follow-up

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5 (RLS), §9 (Configurable dept taxonomy)
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Audit source: `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-wave-a-consistency-audit.md` §D
Severity: P1 (cleanup; not a Wave-B blocker — already hot-fixed inline per audit)

## Goal
Formalize the Reference.Departments RLS contract that was hot-fixed inline during the audit, by adding an integration test that proves cross-org isolation works as specified in `011-departments.sql`. If the inline hot-fix did not actually land in `011-departments.sql`, this task also adds the missing `ENABLE/FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` + `CREATE POLICY` clauses.

## Implementation contract
First verify whether `packages/db/migrations/011-departments.sql` currently contains:
- `alter table "Reference"."Departments" enable row level security;`
- `alter table "Reference"."Departments" force row level security;`
- `create policy departments_org_context on "Reference"."Departments" for all to app_user using (org_id = app.current_org_id()) with check (org_id = app.current_org_id());`

If any of those are missing, add them via a follow-up migration `017-departments-rls-hardening.sql` (do NOT mutate 011 in-place because earlier-applied DBs would not pick up the change). If they are all present, skip the migration step.

Regardless, add an integration test `packages/db/__tests__/departments-rls.integration.test.ts` that:
1. Connects as `app_user` via `getAppConnection()`.
2. Sets org context to a fake org_id.
3. Asserts `SELECT * FROM "Reference"."Departments"` returns 0 rows (the seed is for Apex's org_id only).
4. Sets org context to the Apex org_id (looked up via `getOwnerConnection()` from `organizations`).
5. Asserts `SELECT * FROM "Reference"."Departments"` returns exactly 7 Apex department rows.
6. Attempts `INSERT INTO "Reference"."Departments" (org_id, code, display_name) VALUES (<other-org-id>, 'X', 'Y')` and asserts a 42501 (insufficient_privilege) or RLS-violation error.

If the audit's recommendation was to skip this task because the hot-fix is sufficient, document that decision in the closeout under `deviations_from_prd` and reduce scope to the integration test only.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/migrations/017-departments-rls-hardening.sql [create — only if 011 is missing the RLS clauses]
- packages/db/__tests__/departments-rls.integration.test.ts [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/departments.integration.test.ts [modify — possibly remove duplicate metadata-only RLS check]
- _meta/atomic-tasks/00-foundation/STATUS.md [modify — add 017 to migration ordering lock if created]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration, when runner applies it, then relrowsecurity=true AND relforcerowsecurity=true AND at least one policy exists.
2. Given app_user with non-Apex org context, when SELECT runs, then 0 rows return.
3. Given app_user with Apex org context, when SELECT runs, then exactly 7 rows return.
4. Given app_user INSERT with mismatched org_id, when INSERT runs, then it fails with SQLSTATE 42501 or RLS violation; AND Given the integration test, when pnpm test runs, then it passes.
## Test strategy
- RED: `packages/db/__tests__/departments-rls.integration.test.ts` written before any potential 017 migration; if 011 already has RLS, the test passes immediately and confirms the hot-fix.
- Use the rls.cross-org.integration.test.ts harness as the reference pattern (per audit gold-standard).
- Pin the SQLSTATE for the INSERT failure rather than using bare `.rejects.toThrow()`.

## Risk red lines
- Do NOT modify `011-departments.sql` in-place (idempotency / migration history).
- Do NOT use bare `.rejects.toThrow()` — pin SQLSTATE.
- Do NOT use raw `new pg.Pool` in the test; use `getAppConnection()` and `getOwnerConnection()` (per T-055 lint rule).
- Do NOT skip the test if DATABASE_URL is unset; document the skip with a `// SKIP: requires DATABASE_URL` comment per T-058 convention.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED RLS test before any migration change) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Formalize Reference.Departments RLS via integration test; add 017 follow-up migration only if 011 is missing the RLS clauses.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Verify 011-departments.sql currently has ENABLE/FORCE/CREATE POLICY for Reference.Departments. If yes, skip migration step and add integration test only. If no, add 017-departments-rls-hardening.sql with the missing clauses. Integration test mirrors rls.cross-org pattern with cross-org SELECT/INSERT and pinned SQLSTATE.

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
out_of_scope: Do not modify other Reference.* tables.
acceptance_criteria: Given the migration, when runner applies it, then relrowsecurity=true AND relforcerowsecurity=true AND at least one policy exists.
Given app_user with non-Apex org context, when SELECT runs, then 0 rows return.
Given app_user with Apex org context, when SELECT runs, then exactly 7 rows return.
Given app_user INSERT with mismatched org_id, when INSERT runs, then it fails with SQLSTATE 42501 or RLS violation; AND Given the integration test, when pnpm test runs, then it passes.
test_strategy: RED: write departments-rls.integration.test.ts using rls.cross-org pattern.
risk_red_lines: Do not use raw new pg.Pool.
Do not silently skip without comment.
